channel.title: NHC Atlantic
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Atlantic, Caribbean, and the Gulf of America

item.title: Atlantic Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
151
ABNT20 KNHC 212311
TWOAT

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
800 PM EDT Tue Jul 21 2026

For the North Atlantic...Caribbean Sea and the Gulf of America:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Tropical
Storm Bertha, located over the northeastern Gulf of America.

Tropical cyclone formation is not expected during the next 7 days.

$$
Forecaster Hagen
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Tropical Storm Bertha (AT2/AL022026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...TROPICAL STORM CONDITIONS OCCURRING ALONG PORTIONS OF THE NORTHERN GULF COAST... As of 7:00 PM CDT Tue Jul 21 the center of Bertha was located near 29.3, -87.4 with movement W at 6 mph. The minimum central pressure was 997 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 60 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Bertha
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...TROPICAL STORM CONDITIONS OCCURRING ALONG PORTIONS OF THE NORTHERN GULF COAST...

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Public Advisory Number 10a
item.description:
Issued at 700 PM CDT Tue Jul 21 2026 
000
WTNT32 KNHC 212348
TCPAT2
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Storm Bertha Intermediate Advisory Number 10A
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
700 PM CDT Tue Jul 21 2026
 
...TROPICAL STORM CONDITIONS OCCURRING ALONG PORTIONS OF THE 
NORTHERN GULF COAST...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 700 PM CDT...0000 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...29.3N 87.4W
ABOUT 120 MI...190 KM WSW OF PANAMA CITY FLORIDA
ABOUT 105 MI...170 KM SSE OF MOBILE ALABAMA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...60 MPH...95 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...W OR 270 DEGREES AT 6 MPH...9 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...997 MB...29.44 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
CHANGES WITH THIS ADVISORY:
 
None.

SUMMARY OF WATCHES AND WARNINGS IN EFFECT:
 
A Tropical Storm Warning is in effect for...
* Bay/Gulf County Line in Florida to Morgan City, Louisiana
* Metropolitan New Orleans and Lake Pontchartrain
 
A Tropical Storm Watch is in effect for...
* West of Morgan City, Louisiana, to Cameron, Louisiana
* Lake Maurepas
 
A Tropical Storm Warning means that tropical storm conditions are
expected somewhere within the warning area within 36 hours.
 
A Tropical Storm Watch means that tropical storm conditions are
possible within the watch area, in this case in the next 24 to 48
hours.
 
For storm information specific to your area, including possible
inland watches and warnings, please monitor products issued by your
local National Weather Service forecast office.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 700 PM CDT (0000 UTC), the center of Tropical Storm Bertha was
located near latitude 29.3 North, longitude 87.4 West.  Bertha is
moving toward the west near 6 mph (9 km/h) and a west-northwestward 
or westward motion is expected Wednesday through Friday.  On 
the forecast track, Bertha will move near or along the northern Gulf 
Coast during the next couple of days.
 
Maximum sustained winds are near 60 mph (95 km/h) with higher
gusts.  Little change in strength is expected tonight, and gradual 
weakening is forecast Wednesday through Friday.
 
Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 115 miles (185 km)
from the center.  A wind gust to 43 mph (69 km/h) was recently 
reported at Pensacola Beach, Florida.
 
The minimum central pressure reported by Air Force Reserve and NOAA 
Hurricane Hunter aircraft is 997 mb (29.44 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
Key messages for Tropical Storm Bertha can be found in the Tropical
Cyclone Discussion under AWIPS header MIATCDAT2 and WMO header
WTNT42 KNHC.
 
WIND:  Tropical storm conditions will continue spreading across
portions of the northern Gulf Coast within the warning area tonight
through Wednesday. Tropical storm conditions are possible within
the watch area in Louisiana by Wednesday night.
 
STORM SURGE:  The combination of storm surge and the tide will
cause normally dry areas near the immediate coast to be flooded by
rising waters moving inland from the shoreline. The water could
reach the following heights above ground somewhere in the indicated
areas if the peak surge occurs at the time of high tide...
 
Mouth of the Mississippi River, LA to MS/AL Border...2-4 ft
Cameron, LA to Mouth of the Mississippi River, LA...1-3 ft
MS/AL Border to Aucilla River, FL...1-3 ft
Lake Pontchartrain...1-3 ft
 
The deepest water will occur along the immediate coast.
Surge-related flooding depends on the relative timing of the surge
and the tidal cycle, and can vary greatly over short distances. For
information specific to your area, please see products issued by
your local National Weather Service forecast office.
 
For a complete depiction of areas at risk of storm surge inundation,
please see the National Weather Service Peak Storm Surge Graphic,
available at hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?peakSurge.
 
RAINFALL: Bertha is expected to produce total rainfall amounts of 2
to 4 inches with isolated higher totals around 6 inches through
Thursday along the central Gulf Coast from western Florida into
southern Louisiana. This rainfall may produce isolated flash
flooding, especially in urban areas.
 
For a complete depiction of forecast rainfall associated with
Bertha, please see the National Weather Service Storm Total Rainfall
Graphic available at hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?rainqpf and
the Flash Flood Risk graphic at
hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ero.
 
SURF: Swells generated by Bertha will continue spreading westward
along the northern Gulf Coast during the next few days. These swells
are likely to cause life-threatening surf and rip current
conditions. Please consult products from your local weather office.
 
A depiction of rip current risk for the United States can be found
at: hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ripCurrents
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 1000 PM CDT.
 
$$
Forecaster Pasch
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forecast Advisory Number 10
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC TUE JUL 21 2026 
000
WTNT22 KNHC 212052
TCMAT2
 
TROPICAL STORM BERTHA FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  10
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026
2100 UTC TUE JUL 21 2026
 
TROPICAL STORM CENTER LOCATED NEAR 29.4N  87.2W AT 21/2100Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE NORTHWEST OR 305 DEGREES AT   5 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE  995 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  50 KT WITH GUSTS TO  60 KT.
50 KT.......  0NE  40SE   0SW   0NW.
34 KT....... 70NE 100SE  40SW  40NW.
4 M SEAS.... 45NE 120SE  60SW   0NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 29.4N  87.2W AT 21/2100Z
AT 21/1800Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 29.3N  86.8W
 
FORECAST VALID 22/0600Z 29.6N  87.9W
MAX WIND  45 KT...GUSTS  55 KT.
34 KT... 60NE  90SE  50SW  40NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/1800Z 29.5N  89.3W
MAX WIND  45 KT...GUSTS  55 KT.
34 KT... 50NE  90SE  60SW  30NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/0600Z 29.3N  91.4W...NEAR THE COAST
MAX WIND  40 KT...GUSTS  50 KT.
34 KT... 30NE  60SE  60SW  20NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/1800Z 29.4N  93.4W...OVER WATER
MAX WIND  35 KT...GUSTS  45 KT.
34 KT...  0NE  60SE  40SW   0NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/0600Z 29.8N  95.6W...INLAND
MAX WIND  30 KT...GUSTS  40 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/1800Z 30.3N  97.6W...POST-TROP/INLAND
MAX WIND  25 KT...GUSTS  35 KT.
 
EXTENDED OUTLOOK. NOTE...ERRORS FOR TRACK HAVE AVERAGED NEAR 125 NM
ON DAY 4 AND 175 NM ON DAY 5...AND FOR INTENSITY NEAR 15 KT EACH DAY
 
OUTLOOK VALID 25/1800Z...DISSIPATED
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 29.4N  87.2W
 
INTERMEDIATE PUBLIC ADVISORY...WTNT32 KNHC/MIATCPAT2...AT 22/0000Z
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 22/0300Z
 
$$
FORECASTER REINHART
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forecast Discussion Number 10
item.description:
Issued at 400 PM CDT Tue Jul 21 2026 
000
WTNT42 KNHC 212057
TCDAT2
 
Tropical Storm Bertha Discussion Number  10
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
400 PM CDT Tue Jul 21 2026
 
Bertha remains a sheared tropical storm. The low-level center is 
exposed again, with bursts of deep convection displaced to the south 
by strong northerly shear. Scatterometer data from around 1520 UTC 
indicated that the extent of the tropical-storm-force wind field was 
larger than previous estimates, and the initial wind radii have been 
expanded as a result. Surface observations today showed that 
tropical-storm-force winds spread across some coastal portions of 
the Florida Panhandle. The central pressure has leveled off around 
995 mb based on the last aircraft pass through the center, and the 
intensity is held at 50 kt based on earlier aircraft data. This 
could be a bit generous, but an Air Force Reserve Hurricane Hunter 
mission scheduled for tonight will help reassess the intensity.

After moving northward earlier today, Bertha appears to have resumed 
a northwestward motion (305/5 kt). A mid-level ridge over the 
central United States is expected to build eastward on Wednesday to 
the north of Bertha. This should become the dominant steering 
feature and cause Bertha to move generally westward through midweek. 
This track will take the center of the storm near or along the 
northern Gulf Coast and toward southeastern Louisiana on Wednesday. 
From there, it is still unclear whether Bertha will survive its 
interaction with land and/or redevelop under hostile conditions. The 
GFS and UKMET models show the potential for center redevelopment off 
the coast of Louisiana late Wednesday, while the ECMWF and Canadian 
models favor a more inland solution with steady weakening. A notable 
change is that the track guidance this cycle trended faster, and the 
NHC forecast was sped up between 24-60 h to catch up to the simple 
and multi-model consensus aids. Otherwise, there was little 
cross-track change with this forecast update.

Based on its current structure and unrelenting shear, additional 
strengthening seems unlikely. While convection should continue 
pulsing to the south of Bertha's center, the NHC forecast shows 
gradual weakening during the next 2-3 days in agreement with the 
bulk of the intensity guidance. While this forecast shows Bertha 
surviving as a sheared tropical cyclone across the northern Gulf, it 
remains possible that the system degenerates into a trough over 
southern Louisiana sooner than forecast.

Based on the latest forecast updates, Tropical Storm Watches and 
Warnings have been extended westward along the coast of Louisiana.
 
 
Key Messages:
 
1. Tropical storm conditions are expected to spread westward along 
portions of the northern Gulf Coast from the Florida Panhandle 
westward to southeastern Louisiana, including the Metro New Orleans 
area, through Wednesday night. Tropical storm conditions are 
possible for portions of the southwestern coast of Louisiana 
Wednesday night and Thursday.
 
2. A Coastal Flood Warning is in effect for the Mississippi and 
Louisiana coastlines, where moderate coastal flooding is expected. 
 
3. Interests elsewhere in extreme western Louisiana and along the 
Upper Texas Coast should monitor the progress of this system, as 
additional watches or warnings could be required for portions of 
that area.
 
4. Isolated areas of flash flooding will be possible through 
Thursday along the Gulf Coast from the western Florida Panhandle 
into southern Louisiana.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  21/2100Z 29.4N  87.2W   50 KT  60 MPH
 12H  22/0600Z 29.6N  87.9W   45 KT  50 MPH
 24H  22/1800Z 29.5N  89.3W   45 KT  50 MPH
 36H  23/0600Z 29.3N  91.4W   40 KT  45 MPH...NEAR THE COAST
 48H  23/1800Z 29.4N  93.4W   35 KT  40 MPH...OVER WATER
 60H  24/0600Z 29.8N  95.6W   30 KT  35 MPH...INLAND
 72H  24/1800Z 30.3N  97.6W   25 KT  30 MPH...POST-TROP/INLAND
 96H  25/1800Z...DISSIPATED
 
$$
Forecaster Reinhart
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Wind Speed Probabilities Number 10
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC TUE JUL 21 2026 
000
FONT12 KNHC 212053
PWSAT2
                                                                    
TROPICAL STORM BERTHA WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  10           
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026               
2100 UTC TUE JUL 21 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 2100Z THE CENTER OF TROPICAL STORM BERTHA WAS LOCATED NEAR       
LATITUDE 29.4 NORTH...LONGITUDE 87.2 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED    
WINDS NEAR 50 KTS...60 MPH...95 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   ATLANTIC STANDARD TIME (AST)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   EASTERN  DAYLIGHT TIME (EDT)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   CENTRAL  DAYLIGHT TIME (CDT)...SUBTRACT 5 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        18Z TUE AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       18Z TUE 06Z WED 18Z WED 06Z THU 18Z THU 18Z FRI 18Z SAT
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             06Z WED 18Z WED 06Z THU 18Z THU 18Z FRI 18Z SAT 18Z SUN
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
DESTIN EXEC AP 34  3   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
PENSACOLA FL   34  6   1( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)
 
GFAM 290N 870W 34 99   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)   X(99)
GFAM 290N 870W 50 12   1(13)   X(13)   X(13)   X(13)   X(13)   X(13)
 
MOBILE AL      34  1   3( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)
 
GULFPORT MS    34  X   8( 8)   1( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)
 
STENNIS MS     34  X   5( 5)   3( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)
 
BURAS LA       34  X  27(27)  13(40)   X(40)   X(40)   X(40)   X(40)
 
GFAM 280N 890W 34  X  11(11)   2(13)   X(13)   X(13)   X(13)   X(13)
 
NEW ORLEANS LA 34  X   3( 3)   8(11)   X(11)   X(11)   X(11)   X(11)
 
HOUMA LA       34  X   2( 2)  16(18)   X(18)   X(18)   X(18)   X(18)
 
GFAM 280N 910W 34  X   1( 1)  13(14)   2(16)   X(16)   X(16)   X(16)
 
MORGAN CITY LA 34  X   X( X)  12(12)   2(14)   X(14)   X(14)   X(14)
 
LAFAYETTE LA   34  X   X( X)   2( 2)   3( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)
 
NEW IBERIA LA  34  X   X( X)   5( 5)   3( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)
 
GFAM 280N 930W 34  X   X( X)   3( 3)   9(12)   1(13)   X(13)   X(13)
 
LAKE CHARLES   34  X   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)   2( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)
 
CAMERON LA     34  X   X( X)   2( 2)  18(20)   4(24)   X(24)   X(24)
 
KOUNTZE TX     34  X   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   3( 4)   X( 4)   X( 4)
 
PORT ARTHUR TX 34  X   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   4( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)

GALVESTON TX   50  X   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   2( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
HOUSTON TX     34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   6( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)

PORT O CONNOR  34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   7( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER REINHART
